---

Continue configures MCP servers in YAML. Add a `selvedge` entry under `mcpServers` and the assistant can call Selvedge's tools in agent mode.

## Before you start

`pip install selvedge` gives you the `selvedge` CLI (`selvedge setup`, `selvedge blame`, and the `selvedge prompt` helper below):

```bash
pip install selvedge
```

The MCP config below launches the server with [`uvx`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) instead of a bare `selvedge-server`, so it starts reliably even when your editor doesn't share your shell's PATH â€” it just needs [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/) installed. Prefer the global binary? Swap `uvx --from selvedge selvedge-server` for `selvedge-server`.

## Add the MCP server

Add this to `~/.continue/config.yaml` (global) â€” or a workspace file under `.continue/mcpServers/`:

```yaml
mcpServers:
  - name: selvedge
    command: uvx
    args: ["--from", "selvedge", "selvedge-server"]
```

## Tell your agent when to call it

Selvedge only captures the *why* if your agent knows to call it. Install the one-block instruction set into your project's `CLAUDE.md` / `.cursorrules`:

```bash
selvedge prompt --install CLAUDE.md
```

It's also a copy-paste block on the site: [the Selvedge agent prompt block](/prompt-block/).

## Verify

Switch Continue to **Agent** mode and open its tools list â€” the `selvedge` tools should be available. (MCP tools are only callable in agent/chat-with-tools mode.)

## If it doesn't connect

Continue's YAML config supports stdio servers only (which is all Selvedge needs). Keep the two-space indentation exactly as shown â€” YAML is whitespace-sensitive. The config calls `uvx`, so `uv` must be installed (or `pip install selvedge` and use `command: selvedge-server`).
